title: Insert in Linked List

starterCode.python:
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#    def __init__(self, x):
#        self.val = x
#        self.next = None

class Solution:
    # @param A : head node of linked list
    # @param B : integer
    # @param C : integer
    # @return the head node in the linked list
    def solve(self, A, B, C):

hints: Create a new node with value B.
If the list is empty (A is null), return the new node as the head.
If position C is 0, insert at the head by making new node point to A and return new node.
Otherwise, traverse to position C-1 (0-based indexing).
If you reach the end of the list before reaching C-1, insert at the tail.
Insert the new node by updating pointers: new_node.next = current.next, current.next = new_node.
Return the original head A (unless inserting at head).
